meta.label: Reported-p semantics: near-alpha decision flip is caught (must_detect) while an honoured '<0.001' operator PASSES (must_pass)
meta.ground_truth: synthetic: error planted by construction. (1) A 2x2 contingency table [[16, 34], [25, 25]] recomputes to chi2(1) = 3.35, p = 0.067 (Pearson) / 0.104 (Yates) — both sides of the reported '0.04' sit across alpha = .05, so the significance decision flips: a genuine reporting error the old ratio band (0.067/0.04 = 1.7) silently accepted. (2) A between-group contrast of 20.0 vs 24.0 (SD 2.5, n=60 each) recomputes to p ~ 1e-15, entirely consistent with the reported '<0.001' — the operator must be honoured, not treated as an exact equality a crude ratio test would mis-flag.
meta.notes: Neutral, non-biological. Locks in the shared reported_p_verdict machinery (bug-5 class): decision-flip detection near alpha (must_detect, FLAG — suspicious, not arithmetically impossible) and operator honouring for '<' bounds (must_pass). All numbers are fabricated for the code path; they correspond to no real publication.
